name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union at 850 E Loop 820, Fort Worth, TX offers check-cashing and money transfer services 7 days a week.
description_long:
Western Union at 850 E Loop 820 in Fort Worth, TX is a standalone location offering convenient check-cashing and money transfer services. Located on East Loop 820, this Fort Worth branch operates daily from 7 AM to 9 PM, making it accessible whether you need services early morning or evening. The straightforward storefront layout makes it quick and easy to complete your transactions.

This Fort Worth location provides full Western Union money transfer services, allowing you to send funds domestically and internationally. The 850 E Loop 820 location also handles check-cashing for customers in the Fort Worth area. You can reach the store at +1 817-451-0306 to confirm services or ask about specific fees and requirements.

If you're a Fort Worth resident needing to send money quickly or cash a check, this Loop 820 location offers a reliable option with extended hours. Bring a valid government-issued ID for any transaction. This Western Union in Fort Worth handles both domestic and international transfers with straightforward fees and fast processing.
pros: Massive agent network with 500,000+ locations including partnerships with Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Kroger, and Circle K for convenient access
24/7 online transaction initiation through WU.com, with in-store completion during agent hours providing flexible timing options
Multilingual support in six languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese) serving diverse communities
Real-time transfer tracking allows customers to monitor money movement from initiation to pickup
Multiple service options beyond transfers including money orders, bill payment, mobile top-ups, inmate payments, and prepaid card services
Western Union Rewards loyalty program and referral incentives for repeat customers
Availability in underbanked communities where traditional banking services are limited
cons: Transfer fees are typically higher than online-only alternatives and bank wire services
Exchange rates for international transfers are often less favorable than competitor offerings
Price transparency is limited—specific fees and rates only appear after entering transaction details on the website
Physical location dependency means transaction completion requires visiting an agent during their operating hours
No indication of regulatory oversight details or consumer protection guarantees on the location finder page
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers in communities with limited banking infrastructure
International remittance senders with family or business connections abroad
Customers needing quick money access without a bank account or requiring cash-based transactions
Multilingual speakers in non-English communities seeking financial services in their native language
diagnosis: Western Union is best for unbanked consumers and international remittance senders who prioritize accessibility over cost, with its 500,000+ agent network providing convenient cash pickup and money movement in communities underserved by traditional banking. The primary caveat is that fees and exchange rates are typically higher than online-only competitors, making it less cost-effective for price-conscious users with banking alternatives.
meta_description: Western Union at 850 E Loop 820, Fort Worth, TX. Check cashing and fast money transfers open 7 days a week, 7 AM-9 PM.
